_doc: Founder-OS single source of truth. All UI pages (home, roadmap, projects, tasks, kpis) bind into this file via $state pointers. Fields prefixed with `_doc` or `_doc_` are documentation for the agent — they are ignored by the UI. Do not rename top-level keys. Append-only events go to *.jsonl files. When uncertain about a field, ask the user before writing.
_doc_here_invariant: INVARIANT: `here: true` MUST appear at exactly ONE node across the entire roadmap.phases[*].subs[*] tree. Before setting `here: true` on a new sub, you MUST set/leave `here: false` (or remove the key) on every other sub. Treat this as a hard agent rule — never let two `here: true` exist at the same time.

data._doc_business_profile: Captured during onboarding Q1-Q5. Immutable after onboarding unless explicitly changed by user. product_description / problem_solved / customer_description come from Q5 (free-text parsed into three parts). elevator_pitch holds Q5 verbatim. pmf_substage only set when current_path = 'pmf'.
data._doc_focus: Captured during onboarding Q6-Q8. Updated whenever user reframes priorities (e.g. in Quarterly Rebuild). today = full Q6 answer verbatim. revenue_today / team_size_today / status_one_liner = parsed from Q6 if extractable.
data._doc_current_path: One of: 'pmf' | 'gtm' | 'scale'. Routed during onboarding from business_profile + focus answers. Switched only via explicit Path Switch flow (see SKILL.md).
data._doc_path_state: Path-scoped state for the active path. Replaces the legacy pmf-state.md frontmatter.

path_state._doc_cadences: When recurring operations last fired. next_*_due is computed and used by the master Onboarding Hook to surface 'check-in/measurement/rebuild/annual due' to the user. Scale adds annual cadence (V/TO™ refresh + Wickman Annual Session) which PMF/GTM don't have.
path_state._doc_growth_mode: Captured during scale-onboarding Q-Scale-1. Values: 'bootstrapped' | 'vc_backed' | 'pe_ready'. Drives the auto_fill_recipe Source-Override for step 3.1 (slot 4 swap). Updated during quarterly-rebuild if financial strategy materially shifts (e.g. raised series, pivoted to PE-prep).
path_state._doc_cross_kpis: Three Scale KPIs persisted across all 4 steps. Written monthly by monthly-review.md (Phase D) into cross_kpi_log.template.jsonl AND into this state block. Preserved on step-advance via auto_fill_recipe.md so currentKPI rows carry over. lh_score = Wickman Organizational Checkup™ score (0-100).
path_state._doc_pod_signals: Counters that drive Pod-Cloning-Rule (Schatz): add next pod when capacity-util > 70% AND win-rate ≥ 80% baseline, both sustained ≥ 4 weeks.
path_state._doc_vector_state: Step 3.3 + 3.4: which expansion vectors are active, status of each, kill-criteria-application history. Drives event-3.4-milestone.md fire-condition.
path_state._doc_events: Event flags + history. fired flags are write-once (set by event-* operations). last_fired_at allows events that re-fire (e.g. cac_payback_blowout per quarter). events_pending is consumed by the master Onboarding Hook at the start of the next session.

data._doc_path_state_archive: Preserved path_state from previous paths after a path switch. Keyed by path name (e.g. 'pmf'). Never written to during normal operation.
data._doc_currentFocus: ONE sentence. The single outcome that defines success this phase. Max ~80 chars. No lists, no commas-as-bullets.
data._doc_bottleneck: What is currently blocking progress. title = ≤6 words. description = 1 sentence.
data._doc_oneThing: The ONE highest-leverage action for this week. ≤80 chars, action verb first. Surfaced on home and tasks pages.
data._doc_projects: Projects for the current step. 6–8 per step total. Each: n (rank), title (≤4 words), why (1 sentence), goal (1 sentence with numeric target), outcome (1 sentence), status ('current' | 'later' | 'done'), step (sub_id like '2.1'). 2–3 marked as `current` to actively work on; rest are `later` until promoted.
data._doc_tasks: Concrete tasks for this week. Fields: id (int, unique), text (≤80 chars), done (bool), today (bool — pinned to today), project (matches projects[*].title), date ('today'|'this week'|ISO), dateTone ('today'|'week'). New tasks: increment max id by 1.
data._doc_kpis: Phase-exit metrics. rows: metric (≤24 chars), current (string with unit), target (string with unit), status ('red'|'amber'|'green'). 3–5 rows max.
data._doc_doNotDo: Anti-priorities — things explicitly NOT to do right now (rendered as "Don't do yet" on home.json). 2–4 short imperatives (≤6 words).
data._doc_roadmap: Phase tree. currentSubphase = id of the sub where `here: true`. INVARIANT: exactly one sub across all phases has `here: true`. Mutate by flipping `here` (off the old, on the new); never edit currentFocus directly — it is derived. badge = optional pill rendered next to the roadmap title (e.g. 'First version' during onboarding). Set to null to hide.

subs.name: Step 1a — Survey Design
subs.question: What do users actually feel about the product?
deliverables: Survey link sent to engaged users
subs.exit: Survey is live, link sent to all engaged users.

subs.name: Step 1b — Collection
subs.question: How many responses do we have?

subs.name: Step 1c — Baseline Analysis
subs.question: What is our PMF score and who is our HXC?
subs.exit: Score calculated, HXC extracted, segmentation complete.

subs.name: Step 2 — Roadmap v1
subs.question: What do we build to move the score?
subs.exit: Roadmap has ≥5 initiatives per half, traced to survey data.

subs.name: Operating Mode — Iterate until ≥ 40%
subs.question: Have we reached real product-market fit?
subs.exit: Sean Ellis Score ≥ 40% in 2 consecutive monthly reviews.

subs.name: Find repeatable sales motion
subs.question: Will many people pay?
subs.exit: Same ICP, pain, message, and sales motion repeatedly create paying customers.

subs.name: Prove non-founders can sell
subs.question: Can non-founders sell this?
subs.exit: A non-founder can close customers without the founder.

subs.name: Make it scalable
subs.question: Does this scale?
subs.exit: Sales motion keeps working as volume increases.

subs.name: Ensure customer success
subs.question: Can we scale without churn?
subs.exit: New customers consistently reach value and don't churn.

subs.name: Make it profitable
subs.question: Can we scale profitably?
subs.exit: Growth produces profitable revenue with acceptable CAC, payback, and margin.

subs.name: Hit the gas & scale
subs.question: Does this keep scaling when we hit the gas?
subs.exit: Increasing resources creates more growth without breaking the system.

subs.name: Scale the organization & processes
subs.question: Can the organization handle the growth?
subs.exit: Business operates predictably without every decision flowing through the founder.

subs.name: Expand internationally
subs.question: Can this work in new markets?
subs.exit: At least one new market shows early repeatability.

subs.name: Add additional product lines
subs.question: Can we expand the product without losing focus?
subs.exit: A new product line creates additional revenue without weakening the core.
